units.content: measurer's groups are partitioned by property identifier
at least one property subset where a specified fraction of the measurer's groups are classified as divergent or tension, exceeding a first threshold
at least one property subset where a specified fraction of the measurer's groups are classified as converged, falling below a second threshold distinct from the first
both a divergent-dominant subset and a converged-dominant subset exist for the same measurer simultaneously
the co-existence of both subsets causes emission of a universal-co-divergence signal identifying the measurer together with the divergent and non-divergent property subsets
the universal-co-divergence signal is routed through the feedback-signal interface to the actor
distinguishing divergent from converged property subsets within a single measurer is significant enough to warrant a dedicated signal and actor notification
